project.objective: Who were the parents of John Thomson, the father of George Thomson born in Methlick, Aberdeenshire in 1856?

questions.question: What does John Thomson's 10 January 1856 statutory marriage record in Methlick, Aberdeenshire name as his parents?
questions.rationale: Scottish statutory marriage registers (post-1855) record both parties' fathers and mothers, making this the most direct and accessible evidence for John Thomson's parentage. The marriage date and place are already established from the tree. This is the gatekeeper question: a named father directly answers the research objective and guides all subsequent searches.

exhaustive_declaration.justification: Two independent sources converge on John Thomson (crofter, born ~1789, Methlick area) and Bathia Preslie (born ~1802) as parents of John Thomson (~1826): (1) 1841 Scotland Census household showing John Thomson (crofter) and Bathia Thomson co-residing with child John (~1827) in Methlick, linked by treeMatch L1XD-QD3 to the 1856 marriage record; (2) 1826 OPR marriage showing John Thomson married Bathia Preslie at New Deer on 1 Aug 1826, with treeMatch GKC6-HWW. Marriage date (1826) is consistent with first child John born ~1827. The 1856 statutory marriage register image was inaccessible: FamilySearch has 0 images for this volume; FindMyPast and ScotlandsPeople require human browser access in autonomous mode (URL generated: log_009). This is a pursued-and-unavailable source. The accessible evidence supports a defensible probable conclusion; proof-conclusion will set the honest tier.

stop_criteria.goal_alignment: Two independent records name John Thomson (crofter, ~1789) and Bathia Preslie (~1802) as parents: 1841 census household + 1826 OPR marriage. The specific 1856 marriage image was not read (inaccessible). Conclusion is probable, not proved.
stop_criteria.repository_breadth: FamilySearch exhausted across 5 collections (civil registration, census 1841/1851, OPR births 1564-1950, OPR marriages 1561-1910). FindMyPast 'Scotland Modern and Civil Marriages 1855-2019' and ScotlandsPeople identified as holding the original 1856 register but require human browser access (autonomous limitation). URL generated and logged as log_009.
stop_criteria.original_substitution: Both key findings are from FamilySearch derivative indexes. Originals (ScotlandsPeople/NRS) not accessed due to autonomous mode limitation. The 1856 statutory marriage original would directly name parents — it must be checked by the researcher when browser access is available.
stop_criteria.independent_verification: YES — two genuinely independent sources: (a) 1841 Scotland Census (derivative of NRS schedule; informant: household member + enumerator) and (b) 1826 OPR Marriage (derivative of parish register; informant: minister + parties). Different record types, different institutions, different informants, different creation dates. Both name John Thomson + Bathia as parents.
stop_criteria.evidence_class: Both sources are derivative indexes of original records. Parentage evidence is indirect: (a) household co-residence inferred from household position (no 1841 relationship column); (b) OPR marriage establishes the couple whose first child was born ~1827 (consistent with the research subject). No original source with direct primary parentage information was accessed.
stop_criteria.conflict_resolution: No conflicts detected. The two independent sources are internally consistent: OPR marriage date Aug 1826 → first child born ~1827 (per census) is a plausible 12-month interval. The unique given name Bathia and treeMatch identifiers further corroborate identity linking across sources.
stop_criteria.overturn_risk: Moderate. The 1856 statutory marriage register, if accessed, would either confirm (most likely) or contradict the inferred parents. Risk is moderated by: (1) treeMatch L1XD-QD3 links the 1841 census child John to the 1856 marriage groom; (2) treeMatch GKC6-HWW on the 1826 OPR marriage supports correct identification; (3) the unique given name Bathia is distinctive. Researcher should access ScotlandsPeople register 221/1 or FindMyPast 'Scotland Modern and Civil Marriages' to verify directly before treating as proved.

items.rationale: The Scottish statutory marriage register (post-1855) records the full names of both the groom's and bride's fathers AND mothers, including mother's maiden name. John Thomson married Margaret Simpson on 10 January 1856 in Methlick. Collection 5000163 (Scotland, Civil Registration, 1855–1875, 1881, 1891) is indexed on FamilySearch with 1.5M images. If found, this record directly answers the research question and names John's parents.

ScotlandsPeople (scotlandspeople.gov.uk) is the authoritative repository for Scottish statutory registers and has the original register images for 1856 Methlick marriages. If the FamilySearch index does not return the record, ScotlandsPeople's own index and image viewer will. The statutory marriage entry (register 221/1 as cited in the tree) is known to exist and should be findable here.

In 1841 John Thomson would have been approximately 15 years old and almost certainly living with his parents in the Methlick area. The 1841 Scotland census (FamilySearch collection 2016000, 2.6M records) lists all household members. Co-residence with an adult male Thomson of the right age would be indirect evidence of a parent-child relationship, providing a candidate name to then verify against the marriage register and OPR baptism.

In 1851 John Thomson would have been approximately 25 and unmarried (married 1856). He may still be residing with his parents or working nearby. The 1851 Scotland census (FamilySearch collection 2028673, 2.9M records) would either show him in a parental household or give his birthplace, helping locate his parents. Corroborates parentage identified in the 1856 marriage record.

items.jurisdiction: Methlick and neighboring parishes, Aberdeenshire, Scotland
items.rationale: John Thomson's baptism (born ~1826, Methlick area) would be recorded in the Old Parish Registers (OPR) before civil registration began in 1855. OPR baptism entries name the father (and often mother). FamilySearch collection 1771030 (Scotland, Births and Baptisms, 1564–1950, 8M records) is indexed and covers Aberdeenshire parishes. A baptism entry directly names John's parents and is primary information about the parentage relationship.

items.jurisdiction: Methlick and neighboring parishes, Aberdeenshire, Scotland
items.rationale: ScotlandsPeople holds the original OPR images for Aberdeenshire parishes, including Methlick. If the FamilySearch index does not capture a John Thomson baptism ~1826 (index quality varies by parish), browsing the Methlick OPR images on ScotlandsPeople will reveal entries not indexed elsewhere. This is the authoritative fallback for OPR baptism searches.

items.jurisdiction: Methlick and neighboring parishes, Aberdeenshire, Scotland
items.rationale: Per GPS parentage standards, once candidate parents are identified from the 1856 marriage register or OPR baptism, a dedicated search for the parents' marriage to each other is required. Their OPR marriage (likely 1815–1826) confirms them as a couple, supplies the mother's maiden name (which census records often omit), and provides direct paternity evidence. FamilySearch collection 1771074 (Scotland, Marriages, 1561–1910, 2M records) is indexed. This item is to be executed once candidate parent names are in hand from earlier plan items.

log.notes: 243 results returned; all top-50 ranked at same match score (~6.4) — subject too sparse for discriminating. None clearly identified as John Thomson + Margaret Simpson Methlick 1856 marriage. Records mostly show John Thomson as a parent in other civil registration entries. Proceeding to variant search.

1 result returned but confirmed as false positive: 'Entry for James Adam', a marriage in Aberdeen on 7 Jun 1855 where a different John Thomson is father of the bride (Margaret Thomson married James Adam). Not our John Thomson + Margaret Simpson 1856 Methlick marriage. Collection 5000163 FamilySearch index does not appear to have this specific record indexed as a principal marriage entry. Escalating to broader Scotland Marriages collection 1771074.

Top result (score 8.85, well above all others at 4.56) confirmed as John Thomson + Margaret Simpson, 1856, Methlick, Aberdeenshire: ark:/61903/1:1:XTNY-Q92. This is an index entry — names and date/place confirmed but no parent information in the indexed fields. Image ARK: ark:/61903/1:2:MBVT-QMW. Need to examine the actual statutory register image for parent details. Escalating to image examination.

KEY FINDING: ark:/61903/1:1:VY8C-DK9 (score 0.64, same treeMatch L1XD-QD3 as 1856 marriage record) is John Thomson born ~1827 in the HOUSEHOLD OF JOHN THOMSON (crofter, born ~1789, Methlick) and BATHIA THOMSON (born ~1802). Household also includes children Ann (~1830), George (~1835), William (~1837), Alexr (~1841). This is strong indirect evidence that our John Thomson's father was JOHN THOMSON (crofter, Methlick) and mother was BATHIA (maiden name unknown). Second candidate ark:/61903/1:1:VY8C-8PC (score 0.80, born 1826) is John Thomson as AG LAB farm servant in James Smith's household — may be same person working away, or different individual. Both passed to record-extraction.

31 results returned. Top two ranked matches (scores 0.41): ark:/61903/1:1:VYTC-2Z4 and ark:/61903/1:1:VYTH-GND — both John Thomson born 1826 Aberdeenshire. Previously noted ark:/61903/1:1:VYTD-P5X (treeMatch L1XD-QD3, FARMER 33 ACRES, born 1828) ranks #9 (score 0.074, attached to other). Reading top candidates to identify household context and any parental information.

569 total results; 50 fetched; top 10 ranked. Critical: ark:/61903/1:1:XYFG-9VC (rank 7, score 0.185) has birthDate '7 October 1826' — may be John Thomson's own baptism naming parents. Top 3 (scores 0.45-0.47): ark:/61903/1:1:XYTL-6TG, ark:/61903/1:1:XYTL-D6V, ark:/61903/1:1:XYTL-DRX — all personName 'John Thomson' in OPR, likely the father persona in children's baptism entries (William, George, John Thomson). Reading XYFG-9VC and XYTL-DRX as priority records.

TOP RESULT ark:/61903/1:1:XTNB-Y2W scores 7.05 (all others 4.58 — clear differentiation). CRITICAL FINDING: John Thomson married Bathia Preslie on 1 August 1826 in New Deer, Aberdeenshire. Maiden name Preslie confirmed. Marriage 1 Aug 1826 consistent with first child John born ~1827 (1841 census). New Deer is ~8 miles from Methlick — couple settled in Methlick after marriage. treeMatch GKC6-HWW at 5 stars further supports correct identification. This is the parents' marriage record directly answering the research objective.

5910 results; top result XB47-WXN is John Thomson christened 24 Aug 1828 in Peterhead — wrong parish. Ranker returned subjectResolvable: false (p-john-thomson too sparse for meaningful match scores). All ranked candidates are John Thomson appearing as FATHER in other children's baptism records, not as a baptized child. No clear OPR baptism entry for our John Thomson (~1826, Methlick/New Deer area with parents John + Bathia). The OPR baptism may be unindexed or in ScotlandsPeople only — pli_006 (ScotlandsPeople OPR) remains as fallback.

query.note: Searching for 1856 statutory marriage register entry to obtain parent names of John Thomson and Margaret Simpson. Scottish statutory marriage registers from 1855 include parents' names (including mother's maiden name) for both parties. Also see ScotlandsPeople https://www.scotlandspeople.gov.uk/advanced-search for the original register image (register 221/1, Methlick 1856).

log.notes: URL generated for FindMyPast 'Scotland, Modern and Civil Marriages 1855-2019' targeting John Thomson + Margaret Simpson, Methlick 1856. In autonomous mode, external site access requires human browser interaction — this search cannot be completed without user action. ScotlandsPeople (scotlandspeople.gov.uk) is the authoritative fallback for the original register image. Evidence: (1) same name; (2) wife 'Bathia Preslie' matches 1841 census wife 'Bathia Thomson' (maiden vs. married name); (3) marriage 1826 consistent with eldest child John ~1827 (~12 months post-marriage); (4) New Deer ~8 miles from Methlick — consistent with couple settling in Methlick after marriage; (5) FamilySearch treeMatch GKC6-HWW at 5 stars on the index entry further supports identification.

This is the primary source for I2's maiden name Preslie.

proof_summaries.exhaustive_search_summary: FamilySearch searched across five collections: 1856 civil registration marriage index (collections 5000163 and 1771074), Scotland Census 1841 (collection 2016000), Scotland Census 1851 (collection 2028673), Scotland Births and Baptisms 1564–1950 (collection 1771030, two searches with and without parent name filters), and Scotland Marriages 1561–1910 (collection 1771074). Two key positive findings: (1) 1841 Scotland Census household at Methlick showing John Thomson (Crofter, born ~1789) and Bathia Thomson (born ~1802) with five children including eldest son John (born ~1827), linked by treeMatch L1XD-QD3 to the 1856 marriage record; (2) OPR marriage of John Thomson and Bathia Preslie, 1 August 1826, New Deer, Aberdeenshire, linked by treeMatch GKC6-HWW. Negative findings: 1851 census yielded no definitive match for research subject; OPR baptism searches yielded no clear entry naming parents John and Bathia. The 1856 statutory marriage register image was confirmed as existing (ark:/61903/1:1:XTNY-Q92) but had zero images available on FamilySearch. FindMyPast 'Scotland, Modern and Civil Marriages 1855-2019' and ScotlandsPeople (register 221/1, Methlick) were identified as holding the original register image; a FindMyPast search URL was generated (log_009) but both repositories require authenticated human browser access unavailable in the automated research context.
proof_summaries.narrative_markdown:
## Parents of John Thomson of Methlick, Aberdeenshire — Probable Identification

**Research Question:** Who were the parents of John Thomson (born about 1826, Methlick, Aberdeenshire, Scotland), who married Margaret Simpson on 10 January 1856 in Methlick, Aberdeenshire?

**Conclusion:** The preponderance of available evidence strongly suggests that John Thomson, the 1856 groom of Methlick, was the son of **John Thomson** (born about 1789, Aberdeenshire, occupation Crofter) and **Bathia Preslie** (born about 1801, Aberdeenshire; married surname Thomson), who married on 1 August 1826 in New Deer, Aberdeenshire, Scotland. This conclusion is assessed as **probable**: two independent records converge on this identification, but the most direct source — the 1856 statutory marriage register image — was not accessed, the parentage relationship rests on indirect evidence from derivative sources, and an alternative census candidate was not definitively eliminated.

### The 1841 Scotland Census Household

A search of FamilySearch collection 2016000 located a household at Methlick, Aberdeenshire, headed by John Thomson, identified as a Crofter with an estimated birth year of about 1789. His wife Bathia Thomson (born about 1801) co-resided with five children: John (about 1827), Ann (about 1830), George (about 1835), William (about 1837), and Alexander (about 1841).¹ Adult ages in the 1841 Scotland census were to be rounded down to the nearest five years, so the ages of John and Bathia are estimates. The 1841 census had no relationship column; the parent-child relationship between the household head and the children is inferred from household position, shared surname, and birth-order sequence — indirect evidence.

The eldest child John (born about 1827) in this household is linked by FamilySearch treeMatch identifier L1XD-QD3 to the 1856 statutory marriage entry for John Thomson who married Margaret Simpson in Methlick — the record at the center of this research question. This cross-record association is consistent with the child John in this household being the research subject, though the treeMatch reflects a prior researcher or algorithmic determination rather than a new independent inference.

### The 1826 OPR Marriage at New Deer

A search of FamilySearch collection 1771074 identified a marriage entry scoring 7.05 — substantially above the next result at 4.58 — for **John Thomson** and **Bathia Preslie**, married **1 August 1826** in New Deer, Aberdeenshire, Scotland.² FamilySearch treeMatch identifier GKC6-HWW at five stars is consistent with this identification. This source is a derivative of the original Old Parish Register for New Deer parish, National Records of Scotland. The groom and bride are the informants for their own names (proximity: self; information quality: primary). The officiating minister is the informant for the event date and place (proximity: witness; information quality: primary).

This record supplies the mother's maiden name as **Preslie** — a name that appears as Thomson in the 1841 census after marriage. The given name Bathia is uncommon, reducing the likelihood of misidentification. The marriage date of 1 August 1826 is consistent with the birth of the couple's first child approximately one year later (child John born about 1827 per census). New Deer, Aberdeenshire, lies approximately eight miles northeast of Methlick, consistent with the couple settling in Methlick after their marriage.

### Correlation of the Two Sources

The two sources are genuinely independent: (1) an 1841 government census (household respondent and government enumerator as informants; derivative index of original NRS schedules) and (2) an 1826 Old Parish Register marriage entry (parties and minister as informants; derivative index of original parish register). Different record types, different institutions, different informants, different time periods. Both converge on the same couple: John Thomson (Crofter, Aberdeenshire) and Bathia (maiden name Preslie, married name Thomson) of the Methlick area, with their eldest son John born about 1827. The one-year interval between the 1826 marriage and the ~1827 birth is internally consistent. No contradicting evidence was identified.

### Alternative Census Candidate Considered

The 1841 census search returned two candidates for a John Thomson born approximately 1826 in Aberdeenshire: (1) the household child (record VY8C-DK9, score 0.64) in the Methlick household of John Thomson (Crofter) and Bathia Thomson, bearing treeMatch L1XD-QD3 consistent with the 1856 Methlick marriage entry; and (2) a farm servant (record VY8C-8PC, score 0.80) in James Smith's household, also born approximately 1826 in Aberdeenshire. The farm servant candidate carries a higher raw name/date/place similarity score but critically lacks any cross-record association to the 1856 Methlick marriage. The treeMatch L1XD-QD3 on the household child is the primary discriminator: it reflects a prior FamilySearch determination connecting that record to the 1856 marriage groom. Both interpretations remain possible: (a) the household child is the research subject, with the farm servant being an unrelated John Thomson of similar age; or (b) the farm servant is the research subject working away from his parents' household on census night. This unresolved ambiguity reinforces the **probable** rather than **proved** tier. To resolve it, the researcher should check the farm servant entry (VY8C-8PC) for any links to the 1856 marriage, trace both candidates in the 1851 census, or — most directly — access the 1856 statutory marriage register at ScotlandsPeople.

### Limitation: 1856 Statutory Marriage Record Inaccessible

Scottish statutory marriage registers from 1855 onward record both parties' parents, including the mother's maiden name. The FamilySearch index confirmed the 1856 Methlick marriage entry (ark:/61903/1:1:XTNY-Q92), but no images were available for this volume on FamilySearch. FindMyPast ('Scotland, Modern and Civil Marriages 1855-2019') and ScotlandsPeople (scotlandspeople.gov.uk, Statutory Register of Marriages, Methlick, register 221/1) hold the original register but require authenticated browser access unavailable in the automated context. A FindMyPast URL was generated and logged (log_009) for researcher follow-up. Accessing this record is the single most important next step: it would either confirm the probable parents and advance the conclusion to **proved**, or reveal different parent names requiring a revised hypothesis.

### Negative Evidence

The Scotland Census 1851 returned no definitive match for John Thomson (~1826) in Methlick. The Scotland Births and Baptisms 1564–1950 was searched twice; no OPR baptism naming parents John and Bathia was found for John Thomson (~1826) in Methlick or neighboring parishes. These absences are inconclusive — OPR baptism index coverage varies by parish, and the research subject may appear in an unindexed register.

---
¹ "Scotland, Census, 1841," FamilySearch (https://www.familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:2:9MZD-3MRK : accessed 8 July 2026), entry for John Thomson and Bathia Thomson, Methlick, Aberdeenshire; derivative of original schedules, National Records of Scotland.

² "Scotland, Marriages, 1561–1910," FamilySearch (https://www.familysearch.org/ark:/61903/1:2:MBJL-2VT : accessed 8 July 2026), entry for John Thomson and Bathia Preslie, 1 August 1826, New Deer, Aberdeenshire; derivative of Old Parish Register, New Deer parish, National Records of Scotland.

evaluations.summary: The probable tier call is sound and the honesty about indirect evidence is commendable. Two issues identified and addressed: (1) The competing 1841 census candidate (record VY8C-8PC, farm servant, score 0.80) was not discussed in the original narrative; addressed in ps_001 update with 'Alternative Census Candidate Considered' section. (2) Bathia's birth year 'about 1802' corrected to 'about 1801' (census age 40 → birth 1801). Advisory: treeMatch language softened from 'corroborates' to 'is consistent with'. Recommended next steps: (a) Access ScotlandsPeople register 221/1 (Methlick, 1856) to directly name John's parents and advance to proved; (b) Check farm servant VY8C-8PC in 1851 census to eliminate or confirm as alternative identification.
